text: The following events were reported: Flood, Thunderstorm Wind, Tornado. These occurred near station USW00012916, approximately 29.4319 km away, between 2022-11-26 13:55:00 and 2022-11-26 15:45:00. The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. The events caused property damage valued at 39500.0 and crop damage of 0.0. Episode Narrative: A surface low developed in southeast Texas early November 26th and tracked northeast towards central Arkansas throughout the day. At the same time, a cold front tracked across western Louisiana while a warm front lifted north across southeast Louisiana and southern Mississippi. Moisture rich unstable air within this warm sector over the local area provided the environment to support a few severe thunderstorms. Severe winds, a tornado and flash flooding occurred with this event. A surface low developed in southeast Texas early November 26th and tracked northeast towards central Arkansas throughout the day. Event Narrative: Street flooding reported along Highway 22 in Mandeville. Portion of brick facade blown off residential structure and a tree blown down into power lines in the city of Kenner. A tornado touched down east of Highway 90 and just south of Bayou|Gauche Road, peeling the roof off a house and a car port as well|as flipping a RV. Extensive tree damage was noted around the|property and to the northwest as well as debris thrown a|significant distance. North of Bayou Gauche Road, a church|complex had roof damage to most of the buildings as well as a|flipped RV. These buildings were noted to have also been damaged|from Ida, with blue tarps on the roofs still. One brand new roof,|though, did have notable damage to the southeast corner. As the|tornado moved northwest across Highway 90, mostly tree damage and|light structural damage, such as shingles and siding damage, were|noted before the tornado quickly lifted. Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

A tornado touched down east of Highway 90 and just south of Bayou|Gauche Road, peeling the roof off a house and a car port as well|as flipping a RV. Extensive tree damage was noted around the|property and to the northwest as well as debris thrown a|significant distance. North of Bayou Gauche Road, a church|complex had roof damage to most of the buildings as well as a|flipped RV. These buildings were noted to have also been damaged|from Ida, with blue tarps on the roofs still. One brand new roof,|though, did have notable damage to the southeast corner. As the|tornado moved northwest across Highway 90, mostly tree damage and|light structural damage, such as shingles and siding damage, were|noted before the tornado quickly lifted. Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

At the same time, a cold front tracked across western Louisiana while a warm front lifted north across southeast Louisiana and southern Mississippi. Moisture rich unstable air within this warm sector over the local area provided the environment to support a few severe thunderstorms. Severe winds, a tornado and flash flooding occurred with this event. A surface low developed in southeast Texas early November 26th and tracked northeast towards central Arkansas throughout the day. Event Narrative: Street flooding reported along Highway 22 in Mandeville. Portion of brick facade blown off residential structure and a tree blown down into power lines in the city of Kenner. A tornado touched down east of Highway 90 and just south of Bayou|Gauche Road, peeling the roof off a house and a car port as well|as flipping a RV. Extensive tree damage was noted around the|property and to the northwest as well as debris thrown a|significant distance. North of Bayou Gauche Road, a church|complex had roof damage to most of the buildings as well as a|flipped RV. These buildings were noted to have also been damaged|from Ida, with blue tarps on the roofs still. One brand new roof,|though, did have notable damage to the southeast corner. As the|tornado moved northwest across Highway 90, mostly tree damage and|light structural damage, such as shingles and siding damage, were|noted before the tornado quickly lifted. Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

North of Bayou Gauche Road, a church|complex had roof damage to most of the buildings as well as a|flipped RV. As the|tornado moved northwest across Highway 90, mostly tree damage and|light structural damage, such as shingles and siding damage, were|noted before the tornado quickly lifted. Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

The flood was caused by Heavy Rain. Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. The events caused property damage valued at 39500.0 and crop damage of 0.0. Episode Narrative: A surface low developed in southeast Texas early November 26th and tracked northeast towards central Arkansas throughout the day. At the same time, a cold front tracked across western Louisiana while a warm front lifted north across southeast Louisiana and southern Mississippi. Moisture rich unstable air within this warm sector over the local area provided the environment to support a few severe thunderstorms. Severe winds, a tornado and flash flooding occurred with this event. A surface low developed in southeast Texas early November 26th and tracked northeast towards central Arkansas throughout the day. Event Narrative: Street flooding reported along Highway 22 in Mandeville. Portion of brick facade blown off residential structure and a tree blown down into power lines in the city of Kenner. A tornado touched down east of Highway 90 and just south of Bayou|Gauche Road, peeling the roof off a house and a car port as well|as flipping a RV. Extensive tree damage was noted around the|property and to the northwest as well as debris thrown a|significant distance. North of Bayou Gauche Road, a church|complex had roof damage to most of the buildings as well as a|flipped RV. These buildings were noted to have also been damaged|from Ida, with blue tarps on the roofs still. One brand new roof,|though, did have notable damage to the southeast corner. As the|tornado moved northwest across Highway 90, mostly tree damage and|light structural damage, such as shingles and siding damage, were|noted before the tornado quickly lifted. Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

A tornado touched down east of Highway 90 and just south of Bayou|Gauche Road, peeling the roof off a house and a car port as well|as flipping a RV. Extensive tree damage was noted around the|property and to the northwest as well as debris thrown a|significant distance. North of Bayou Gauche Road, a church|complex had roof damage to most of the buildings as well as a|flipped RV. These buildings were noted to have also been damaged|from Ida, with blue tarps on the roofs still. One brand new roof,|though, did have notable damage to the southeast corner. As the|tornado moved northwest across Highway 90, mostly tree damage and|light structural damage, such as shingles and siding damage, were|noted before the tornado quickly lifted. Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.

Law enforcement reports of tree damage and snapped power pole near Lockport.
